mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 3.64% combined with month-over-month CPI acceleration signals the Fed is in active tightening mode. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 3.5% drives 10-year Treasury yields upward as market reprices long-duration risk and inflation expectations remain sticky over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed in active tightening cycle as of June 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 10 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and CPI acceleration, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.3% signals tight labor market. Sustained low UNRATE below 4.5% combined with elevated PAYEMS growth indicates wage pressure persists, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy stance. This expectation of continued rate hikes pushes 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market remains tight at 4.3% unemployment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points despite UNRATE remaining below 4.5%, or forward returns show no significant correlation with UNRATE levels (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1502K units combined with retail sales at 757B indicate demand-side resilience. However, this demand persistence in the face of Fed tightening (FEDFUNDS 3.64%) creates inflation expectations that push 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices in longer duration of restrictive policy needed to cool demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Demand remains elevated despite monetary tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 20 basis points despite sustained demand signals and elevated FEDFUNDS, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 102.5 (index 2017=100) shows supply-side constraints persist. Elevated INDPRO combined with sticky inflation (CPIAUCSL 332.4) signals supply-demand imbalance. This imbalance forces the Fed to maintain hawkish stance, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as real yields must rise to suppress demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Supply constraints persist in manufacturing sector
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite INDPRO elevation and CPI persistence, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL is not significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 332.4 represents cumulative inflation pressure. When CPI month-over-month growth exceeds 0.6%, market reprices terminal Fed funds rate expectations upward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to spike within 5 trading days as forward guidance expectations shift.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI remains elevated at 332.4 index level
regime_hint: high-inflation regime requiring aggressive Fed response
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 5 basis points within 5 trading days following CPI print > 0.6%, or Rank IC < 0.08 over any rolling 6-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 24174.5B (last quarterly reading) combined with sustained retail sales (RSAFS 757B) indicates demand remains above trend. This above-trend demand growth forces the Fed to tighten more aggressively, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher as the market extends the duration of the tightening cycle.
regime_hint: above-trend demand growth in tightening cycle
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 30 basis points over 60 trading days despite sustained demand signals, or correlation between RSAFS and forward 60-day yield changes is not significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 158.7M combined with unemployment at 4.3% creates a regime where labor market tightness forces wage growth. Sustained PAYEMS growth above 150K monthly combined with UNRATE below 4.5% signals the Fed must maintain restrictive policy longer, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as the market prices extended tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market remains tight with strong employment
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 12 basis points over 20 trading days despite PAYEMS > 158K and UNRATE < 4.5%, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 102.5 signals supply-side constraints remain binding. When INDPRO fails to grow month-over-month (flat or declining), combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 3.64%, the market reprices expectations: supply constraints prevent demand destruction, forcing the Fed to raise rates further, pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Supply constraints persist despite Fed tightening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days despite weak INDPRO growth and elevated FEDFUNDS, or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.15) in rolling regressions.
